item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images used in this article are cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0), as the first image comes from here licensed under CC BY 3.0, and the second image is from Wikimedia Commons.

item.title: Staff Post- Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show
- hide

To link to the main Pitch Haven Hub, I propose the following edit:

Researchers at Site-45 and personnel stationed at SCP-2988 have been instructed to report new findings/discoveries about SCP-2988's origins

to

Researchers at Site-45 and personnel stationed at SCP-2988 have been instructed to report new findings/discoveries about SCP-2988's origins

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do you remember me? I'm the Chinese translator of your Red Reality. Is today, I suddenly found that I has translated your work long time ago.

let me see… 27 Jun 2015, what a long time. Fate is really wonderful.

(((This is the official version.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: About damn time!
WOOOOO My little hand tree of death finally hit 100!! Go Izaak, go! Go Izaak, go!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty late reply, but thank you so much! ^^ This is definitely my favorite thing I've written after my dragons, so really glad you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still satisfying after another read through. Excellent imagery. Wonderfully dark story. My favorite of the Pitch Haven series. This could use more love.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoa, just realized I missed the first year anniversary! >_>

Happy anniversay, Izaak!! Give the tree a tree and hyena a hand! huehuehuehuehue

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NEVER teleport your problems to a destination you're not familiar with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, i just got introduced to Pitch-Haven, and its rather immersive! This is a very creepy skip, and can hold it's own which is great!

What got me to +1 was the inclusion of "original" text, from whatever means crafting an imaginary language isn't easy. Regardless if it's a language generator available online, the extra effort is a nice touch!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed the article and upvoted but I'm also not a fan of the picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did! And might I say, this is an awesome piece, and I am elated to have this up for PH. Excellent work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Update: Big thank you to Fantem for providing the original A-12 Documents for the Addenda!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you liked it! Hope you enjoy the rest of what Pitch Haven has to offer as well!

In regards to the tabs, Fantem and I brainstormed and thought tabs would emulate a bit of the feeling/suspense you might read from 2746. I did play around with collapsibles in the earlier drafting stages and it felt/looked… kind of odd (Putting all the collapsibles together without a break made the page look somewhat empty at the bottom); I found it refreshing to have tabs for the addenda as a replacement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Am now more than a bit curious about this Pitch Haven thing; I'm on to 2746 next.

I REALLY like this. This tree IS the kind of thing the Foundation is here to secure.

spoilers
just what I've cobbled together

The fact that this was started by a spellcasting hyena gives it something of a sorceror's appprentice feel to it. His descent from wanting to help to actively baiting victims to the tree was fantastically creepy (and believable).

My only critique is in the use of tabs. This is mostly a personal thing, but I find tabs work great for short/one screen blocks of information; having to scroll back up for lengthy passages "disconnects" you from the work for a moment. Maybe I'm just too used to collapsibles.

+1. Looking forward to more!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted as soon as I saw the picture, and the article only reinforced it more. Good job on writing an extremely creepy article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay. This is very good work, Goomy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really? Other people who looked at it in draft stage said it was pretty fitting and captured the Pitch Haven-esque feel. To each their own, though I feel you might be judging a book by its cover, for lack of a better phrase.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the suggestions; they've been made accordingly.

I've contacted Fantem about how A-12 translation would work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image is unconvincing. Couldn't be arsed to read the rest because of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I notice you have inconsistent spacing, and can't tell whether you're double-spacing after each sentence, or not. Also:

They can travel approximately thirty meters from SCP-2988 while in pursuit before showing signs of losing mobility. (i.e. slowing of speed, collapse)

I feel like the italics would fit better in a footnote.

(See addendum below for more information.)

I don't think this is needed at all, as the reader will quickly arrive at that addendum.

As a final quibble, will you or Fantem write out Part D in the original language, a la 2746?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it when I read the draft, love it here too +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From someone with no real background on Pitch Haven (read 2746, haven't gone much further) - I just want to say that this is terrific even without the full context. Writing cognitive dissonance is incredibly hard, and the diary scraps do a very good job. A great way to expand on what was already pleasantly off-putting.

A few scraps I noticed
Which you're welcome to ignore

Researchers at Site-45 have been instructed to report new findings/discoveries about SCP-2988's origins, as well information on POI Izaak, to personnel stationed at SCP-2988.

"as well as information on POI Izaak"?

The same goes for the reverse as well.

This feels like a tone break. Could you rephrase the whole section to read: "Researchers at Site-45 and personnel stationed at SCP-2988 have been instructed to share new findings/discoveries about SCP-2988's origins, as well information on POI Izaak."

SCP-2988 removed four pairs of limbs (not necessarily matching species)

I found "not necessarily" confusing here, as this was a report of the first time SCP-2988 had taken this action. Could you live with "removed eight limbs of different species, without an intended target. The limbs proceeded to travel in pairs to the thirty meter border line…"

Ongoing collaborative research in both Site-45 and SCP-2988's containment site.

I feel like this sentence is lacking a verb. Do you mean "Collaborative research is ongoing in both…"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this article. Pitch Haven has always been one of the more middling canons for me, but this really brings some of the horror in what happened there home in a very real and sympathetic way. It's not hard to sympathise with the villain protagonist of the documents, which is really just icing on the cake of an already creepy and effective scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With the revelation that the Edenites made the fruit sterile (and thus, the tree throwing fruit was futile), I like that the tree was all "Okay, fine! I'll just do cuttings/transplants instead!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for catching that! Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is an SCP-X-1 reference in the second paragraph of the Descripcion and SCP-X on the Image. I'm guessing they are from the Draft.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I said it in chat, but it bears repeating; I love this. In my opinion, you've nailed the Pitch Haven feel, the subtleties to this are neat, and, for what's essentially a reasonably simple idea, you've pulled it off well. Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to thank everyone who helped review this, in both chat and forums. Shout-outs to DreamWalkerFae, TwistedGears, Baby_Boo, nulljellyfish, TyGently, Iceman76, MrWrong, Dr_Leonard, and SoundChaser.

Also, a HUGE thank you to Fantem for their endless support and answering of my questions with the Pitch Haven Series, and for being the original creator of the Pitch Haven Series to begin with.

This SCP was a long time in the making, and I hope you enjoy it.
